Functional Modules.Function Description: Manages the single-cell battery charging, provides a stable logic voltage rail using a step-up followed by a step-down regulation topology to withstand severe battery voltage drops during motor activations.
Functional Modules.Signal Sources: Single-cell LiPo battery, external charger input.
Functional Modules.Implementation Logic: Integrated charging IC for the battery. A boost converter first raises the variable battery voltage to a level above the required logic voltage, then a buck converter regulates it down to a precise, stable Vlogic (e.g., 3.3V), ensuring continuous operation during load transients.

Functional Modules.Function Description: The main system controller. Aggregates sensor data, executes flight/stabilization algorithms, generates timed pulse signals for motor control, and manages system states.
Functional Modules.Signal Sources: Sensor Suite (via I2C/SPI), Diagnostic Bridge (UART/Boot control).
Functional Modules.Signal Destinations: High-Current Drive Channels (PWM signals), Diagnostic Bridge (communication).
Functional Modules.Implementation Logic: A microcontroller (MCU) or microprocessor with integrated wireless capability (e.g., Bluetooth/Wi-Fi). Runs the main firmware, processing sensor fusion data and control loops to output PWM commands.

Functional Modules.Function Description: Provides real-time orientation and altitude data. Includes a multi-axis IMU (gyroscope, accelerometer) and a barometric pressure sensor.
Functional Modules.Implementation Logic: A combined IMU+pressure sensor module or discrete ICs communicating via I2C or SPI. The IMU provides angular rate and linear acceleration. The pressure sensor provides altitude estimation.

Functional Modules.Function Description: Drives external inductive loads (e.g., brushless DC motors) based on CPU commands. Provides high-current capacity for motor control.
Functional Modules.Implementation Logic: Motor driver ICs or discrete MOSFET bridges (e.g., 3-phase for BLDC). Gate drivers amplify CPU PWM signals to switch high-current MOSFETs, controlling motor speed and direction.

Functional Modules.Function Description: Handles external wired communication (e.g., for telemetry) and facilitates seamless firmware flashing by automatically forcing the main CPU into bootloader/programming mode.
Functional Modules.Signal Destinations: Wireless CPU (UART, boot mode control pin).
Functional Modules.Implementation Logic: A USB-to-serial bridge IC (e.g., CP2102, CH340) or a dedicated communication MCU. It monitors the communication line; upon detecting a specific command or sequence from the host, it toggles a hardware pin (e.g., MCU reset/boot) to force the main CPU into its bootloader, enabling firmware updates without user button presses.
